body.u: I left school at sixteen- I was slightly under sixteen actually with five o’levels. I had provisional entry to the College of Technology at Maidstone and only needed four o’levels to get in.

I went to college and spent two years doing a Business Studies course. I had a good time there- it was a lovely place- and left with an Ordinary National Diploma in Business Studies. This opened up many possibilities for me, such as managerial schemes run by firms, accountancy, banking and so on. All I wanted to be was a personal secretary so I applied for posts in Maidstone and Sheerness. I did think of London but it was too far to travel and I didn’t fancy living in.
Anyway I got the job in Sheerness at the Olau Line ferry company as a junior secretary. I left there after nearly a year and went to work as a personal secretary in Maidstone.
It was here that I met my husband. I had been working there for over a year when I met him. He’d been with the turn for a couple of years and had just been promoted.
We started going out together and after about a year we got married.
I didn’t give up work altogether but took a part time job with the firm instead.
That had to stop, though, when we had our first child, Christine. She was a lovely little baby and really looked like her dad.
She was two when Michael came along.
By this time our house was getting crowded and rather than move, we decided to extend. That done, we had another boy. When he was five my husband prompted me to take another job so I did.
The kids grew up really fast and it seemed like no time until Chris got married. She was 22 and at 25 had her first child, my first grandchild.
Mike remained single until he was nearly 29 because he had joined the Navy. However, he got married and provided me with grandchildren, as well.
Chris and her husband had another girl and decided to limit the family to two.
Actually we did talk over the possibility of having another child ourselves but decided not to because, as my husband put it, we couldn’t stand all that again!
By this time the other boy John was doing very well for himself. He had become area manager to some firm or other and was working very hard. He got married at 26 and had a family of his own.
Now my first grandchild was nearly 10 and the other were growing up fast!
Now I sit here with them all around me at the ripe old age of 69 and remenise. I can remember little facts that I thought I had forgotten, over the years.
